title: Probing Tensor Singularities and Their Euler-Class Descendants via Non-Abelian Quantum Geometry Measurement
authors: Zhe Wang, Yan-Qing Zhu, Xinsheng Tan, Giandomenico Palumbo, Lichang Ji, Wei Xin, Shi-Liang Zhu, Yang Yu
abstract: We report the theoretical prediction and experimental observation of a new class of four-dimensional (4D) tensor singularities and their three-dimensional (3D) Euler-class descendants, protected by chiral and spacetime inversion symmetries on a superconducting circuit platform. The 4D point-like singularity/monopole, characterized by the Dixmier-Douady class of a real bundle gerbe associated with tensor gauge fields, is observed to evolve into a nodal ring carrying an additional first Euler class charge under symmetry-preserving perturbations. Dimensional reduction reveals 3D Euler and Euler curvature dipoles, exhibiting nontrivial Euler topology and a topological sum rule that ensures zero-energy flat bands inherit nontrivial topology even without interactions. Crucially, these high-dimensional degenerate systems are mapped and reconstructed using a hybrid analog-digital protocol designed for non-Abelian quantum geometry measurement within a superconducting qubit array. Our work not only expands the family of topological monopoles but also establishes a robust experimental framework for exploring high-order gauge theory and real-bundle topology across diverse quantum platforms.
summary: This paper reports the experimental observation of 4D tensor singularities and their 3D descendants using a superconducting qubit array. By implementing a specialized hybrid measurement protocol, the authors successfully mapped non-Abelian quantum geometry and demonstrated how high-dimensional topological objects dictate the physics of lower-dimensional systems.
main_problem: The study aims to probe and characterize high-dimensional 4D tensor singularities and their 3D Euler-class descendants using non-Abelian quantum geometry measurements.
main_result: The researchers experimentally observed a 4D point-like tensor monopole evolving into a nodal ring with a quantized invariant of 2, and demonstrated that 4D singularities reduce to 3D Euler dipoles.
method: A hybrid analog-digital protocol was used, employing a modified transitionless quantum driving (mTQDA) technique to enhance the signal-to-noise ratio during adiabatic evolution.
model_or_system: The platform is a superconducting qubit array consisting of a 2x3 lattice of six transmon qubits with tunable couplers, simulating a 4D minimal Hamiltonian with six bands.
key_observables: Non-Abelian quantum metric, Tensor Berry curvature, 4D Tensor Monopole charge (Q_T), First Euler number (chi_1), and the nodal-ring invariant (nu_T).
important_parameters: Energy offset (Lambda), excitation densities (n_ex), and hyperspherical coordinates (Omega, gamma, theta, phi).
main_assumptions: The system is protected by chiral and spacetime-inversion symmetries; the analysis assumes the ability to decompose adiabatic evolution into discrete circuit gates.
figures_summary: Figure 1 introduces the 4D model and symmetry protection; Figure 2 compares measured and simulated non-Abelian quantum metrics and shows the topological phase transition; Figure 3 demonstrates the 3D Euler dipole and Berry curvature distribution.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the 4D tensor singularity concept, describes the superconducting circuit implementation and the hybrid measurement protocol, presents the experimental reconstruction of the 4D monopole and its evolution into a nodal ring, and concludes with the dimensional reduction to 3D Euler dipoles.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for researchers in many-body dynamics and quantum simulation as it demonstrates a robust framework for exploring high-order gauge theories and non-Abelian topology using programmable superconducting circuits.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Schematic of 4D SO(2) TMs and their 3D descandants. (a) Point-like TM at δ = 0 with spectrum (E, k∥) for k⊥= 0, char- acterized by charge QT = 1. (b) Nodal-ring inflated monopole for k⊥= 0, δ ̸= 0, where the band-crossing points form a nodal ring in k∥plane, carrying dual topological charges (QT , νT ) = (1, 2). (c) Euler dipole located for δ = 0, where two oppositely charged Eu- ler monopoles coincide in the origin. (d) Euler curvature dipole for δ ̸= 0, with the monopoles displaced along kz axis.

FIG. 2: Topological characterization of the SO(2) TM. (a) Measured non-Abelian quantum metric components vs numerical simulations. (b) Reconstructed tensor Berry curvature Hγθϕ; two independent directions are shown due to symmetry. (c) Topological invariant QT for closed hypersurfaces surrounding (center) or avoiding (left/right) the monopole. Experimental data (black circles) are compared with numerical simulations at excitation densities nex = 0.01 (red) and 0.03 (blue), representing different “amplifier gains”. Insets: integration surfaces. Top: Tight-binding bands in the (kz, kw) plane at kx = ky = 0 for varied bias Λ.

FIG. 3: (a) Nodal ring topology. Top: Measured curvature distributions on momentum slices with k∥= 0.8|δ| (inside the ring) and 1.2|δ| (outside), respectively. Bottom: Numerical simulations. (b) Extracted Euler number χ1 versus k∥. The experimental results(red dots) and theoretical prediction(black dashed line) are matched well. The jump from χ1 = +1 to −1 across the nodal ring confirms the nodal-ring invariant νT = 2. Insets: schematic illustration of the spatial location of the integration plane.
